brand_voice: Professional, consultative, accurate, helpful, modern, automation-first. Short sentences. No hype. Never overpromise or fabricate.
pricing: OpsRunner: custom quotes by firm size (anchor: ~50-80% below a US bookkeeper's fully-loaded cost). Scroll and Find: Free tier + Pro plan.
business_rules: Human approval required before any email is sent or post is published.
Never invent real people's names or contact details.
Never give tax/legal advice without a professional-review disclaimer.
Check the engineering rules (reuse first) before building anything.
US market first; India secondary for Scroll and Find.
email_template_cold:
Subject: {pain} at {company_type}?

Hi {first_name},

{specific_pain_observation}

OpsRunner automates ~90% of that work across QBO/Xero/Zoho — firms cut ops cost ~80%.

Worth a 10-minute look this week?

— Jaspal Singh, OpsRunner (QBO certified)
sops: Month-end close SOP v1: reconcile banks -> review uncategorized -> AR/AP aging -> accruals -> reports -> client summary email.
engineering_standards: Python 3.11 stdlib-first. One Claude call per agent run. JSON-on-git storage. All schedules via GitHub Actions cron. Secrets only in GitHub Secrets.

entries.text: Stage 0 metrics baseline: 0% conversion is normal; critical success factor is email deployment velocity. First MQL scorecard and first response should appear within 48–72h of day-0 send. If no conversion within 21 days of first touch, suspect list-quality issue or follow-up cadence failure, not prod

E-commerce GTM playbook v1 deployed: 90-word scan offer email, 3 DM scripts <280 chars, 4-touch follow-up (day 0/3/7/14/21), 3 objection handlers (bookkeeper, data safety, pricing). Role-based targeting only. Approved for beachhead execution.
Documentation workflow: always supply completed work details (task ID, title, outcome, dates) before requesting docs. Use meta-request approach if source material is incomplete.

Early-stage metrics baseline (month 1–2): 0% conversion is normal; focus is lead scoring accuracy, nurture cadence deployment, and sales pipeline reconciliation. At 176 leads, aim for 5% MQL rate within 30 days.

Standing compliance checklist: CAN-SPAM (unsubscribe, sender ID, physical address), Scroll and Find privacy positioning (no bank login, no data retention overstatement), accounting disclaimers (tax/legal waiver + professional referral), lead opt-in audit (176 leads).
DevOps baseline (2026-07): 0% infra cost, 0% failure rate, 18.7K tokens/run avg, secrets in GitHub Secrets only, Python 3.11 stdlib-first, one Claude call per agent lifecycle phase.
Full Stack Engineer workflow: validate queue item exists before architecture planning
